title: Capital Structure: If a firm increases its debt, the expected return on...
summary: Assess how increasing debt affects expected return on equity under Modigliani-Miller.
prompt: If a firm increases its debt, the expected return on equity will decrease as per the Modigliani-Miller theorem.
solution: According to Modigliani-Miller, increasing debt increases the financial risk to equity holders, which typically raises the expected return on equity due to the increased risk premium demanded by investors.
